pattern: Sort by left endpoint ascending then by right endpoint descending; sweep with a running maximum right boundary and count intervals not swallowed by it.
hints: An interval is covered when another interval starts no later and ends no earlier; you need to count how many are NOT covered.
Sort so that when you reach an interval you can compare its right endpoint against the largest right endpoint seen among earlier intervals with the same or smaller left endpoint.

After sorting by (left ascending, right descending), track the farthest right endpoint seen. If the current interval's right exceeds that maximum, it is not covered — increment the count and update the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: two identical intervals, one covers the other
sharing only the left endpoint does not imply coverage unless right also covered
nested chain where each is covered by the outermost
no interval covers another; all survive
example from problem statement with one covered interval

stress.description: random intervals over a moderate value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    ivs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randint(0, 99990)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, 100000)
        ivs.append([a, b])
    return [ivs]
